articles.full_text:
Uttarakhand weather: Heatwave intensifies, Dehradun records 29.7°C, second hottest March in 10 years
March has begun with scorching heat in Uttarakhand. Dehradun recorded 29.7°C on March 1, nearly 6 degrees above normal. Meteorologists say this is the second hottest March day in 10 years, highlighting climate change and unusual weather patterns in the Himalayan state.
Dehradun: Uttarakhand is facing an unusual rise in temperatures as March begins with a heatwave. On Sunday, March 1, the state capital Dehradun recorded a maximum temperature of 29.7 degrees Celsius, nearly 6 degrees above the seasonal average. Meteorologists say this is the second hottest March day in the last decade.
According to weather experts, the only hotter March day in recent years was March 26, 2021, which remains the all-time record. The current temperature is considered unusually high for the beginning of March, pointing to changing climate patterns and increasing heat stress in the region.
No rainfall in January-February
Meteorologists explained that the absence of rainfall in January and February has contributed to the rising heat. Normally, winter showers help maintain soil moisture and keep temperatures balanced. However, this year, the lack of rain has reduced soil moisture, leading to higher day and night temperatures. Both daytime and nighttime readings are consistently above normal, intensifying the heatwave.
Climate change concerns
Experts warn that the continuous rise in temperature is a clear sign of climate change. The unusual heat in early March reflects shifting weather patterns, which may bring more extreme conditions in the coming months. If rainfall does not improve, the heat could intensify further, setting new records.
Forecast ahead
According to the Meteorological Center in Dehradun, dry weather will continue across Uttarakhand until March 6. During this period, temperatures are expected to rise by another 6 to 7 degrees Celsius. However, relief may arrive on March 7, when cloudy skies and light rain are predicted in the mountainous regions. This could bring partial relief to the plains as well.
Heat across the state
The rising heat is not limited to Dehradun. Other districts in Uttarakhand are also experiencing above-normal temperatures. Residents are already feeling the discomfort of summer-like conditions in early March. Experts advise people to stay hydrated, avoid direct exposure to the sun, and prepare for hotter days ahead.
With the heatwave intensifying, Uttarakhand is witnessing one of its hottest starts to March in recent memory. The situation highlights the urgent need to address climate change and prepare communities for shifting weather patterns in the Himalayan region.
